text.title: JUnit Testing Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for naming, structure, layer assignment, and coverage thresholds in Spring Boot JUnit 5 tests.

rule.statement: Name every test method as methodUnderTest_state_expectedBehavior.
rule.rationale: Self-documenting failure output in CI without reading the test body. Example: findById_whenUserNotExists_throwsException.

rule.statement: One test class per SUT: UserService maps to UserServiceTest in the same package.
rule.rationale: Agents searching for tests for UserManager named UserManagerTests waste cycles; package-private access requires matching package.

rule.statement: Every test method must have visible Arrange / Act / Assert sections separated by blank lines.
rule.rationale: Agents emit clean tests when prompted with structure; reviewers spot assertion-free tests immediately.

rule.statement: Use AssertJ for all non-trivial assertions; never use JUnit assertEquals for collections or extracted fields.
rule.rationale: assertThat(list).extracting(User::getEmail).containsExactly(...) beats hand-rolled loops and produces clear diff on failure.

rule.statement: Controller tests use @WebMvcTest(SUT.class) with @MockBean on the service; never boot @SpringBootTest for controller-only tests.
rule.rationale: @WebMvcTest slices load only the web layer; @SpringBootTest loads the full context — 5-10x slower for no benefit at this boundary.

rule.statement: Service tests use @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) with @InjectMocks and @Mock; no Spring context.
rule.rationale: Spring context is irrelevant for plain service logic; plain Mockito is 100x faster and does not pollute the context cache.

rule.statement: Repository tests use @DataJpaTest with Testcontainers; never use H2 for behavior parity with Postgres.
rule.rationale: H2 dialect diverges from Postgres on JSON columns, locking behavior, and function availability; tests pass on H2 and fail in prod.

rule.statement: Use exactly one @SpringBootTest per test module for full-stack integration; do not scatter it across suites.
rule.rationale: Each distinct @MockBean set invalidates Spring's context cache; spreading @SpringBootTest multiplies boot cost.

rule.statement: Enforce line >= 70% and branch >= 60% via Jacoco in CI; fail the build when thresholds drop.
rule.rationale: Coverage without a gate is advisory only; agents skip uncovered paths unless the build blocks.

rule.statement: Run mutation tests (Pitest) on payment, auth, and billing paths; coverage alone hides assertion-free tests.
rule.rationale: A test that calls service.foo() and verifies nothing still counts toward coverage; Pitest finds surviving mutations.

rule.statement: Never use Thread.sleep in tests; use Awaitility.await().atMost(...) for async assertions.
rule.rationale: Thread.sleep is flaky under load; Awaitility polls with configurable interval and timeout, failing fast on success.

rule.statement: Inject a Clock bean and assert exact equality; never use assertThat(ts).isCloseTo(now(), within(1, SECONDS)).
rule.rationale: Time window assertions flake under CI load; injectable Clock makes time deterministic in tests.

text.reference: CI script to parse jacoco.xml and enforce line/branch thresholds
